seo_h1: Salmanas the Alchemist on the Craft of Spherical Hail

seo.browser_title: The Salmanas the Alchemist on Eulogikon Greek Archive
seo.meta_title: Classical Salmanas the Alchemist Ancient Greek Treatise
seo.meta_description: Salmanas the Alchemist, a late antique Greco-Egyptian alchemist. His treatise explores alchemical science. Free to read and download from the Greek archive.

biography_html:
Salmanas the Alchemist is a name attributed to a figure from the late antique period, likely between the 3rd and 5th centuries CE. He is associated with the Greco-Egyptian tradition of alchemy, a field that combined practical craft, philosophical theory, and spiritual ideas.

No reliable historical records or biographical details about an individual named Salmanas exist in standard references for ancient Greek authors. No specific writings are definitively credited to him. The primary surviving corpus of Greek alchemical texts features other authors, and no treatise under the name Salmanas has been identified.

According to modern scholars, the name may be a pseudonym, a corrupted version of another name like Solomon, or belong to a minor figure whose work is entirely lost. If Salmanas was a historical practitioner, his significance would lie within the broader study of late antique alchemy. This tradition is important for the history of science and ideas, representing early experimentation with materials and the development of technical language. Without any attested works, however, his personal contribution cannot be established from available sources.

title.english: Method by Which Spherical Hail is Made by the Renowned Craftsman Arabus Salmanas
title.ancient_greek: Μέθοδος δι’ ἧς ἀποτελεῖται ἡ σφαιροειδὴς χάλαζα
